steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two sculptures. The relevant facts from the notes: Kosuth’s sculpture spells out a phrase; Dawood’s forms an abstract moon shape. A good answer must make that contrast clear and central — nothing else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘neon lighting has a number of different applications’: buries the difference between the two sculptures inside a three-part list about advertising history. The goal is to emphasize the difference, not list applications of neon. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘one spells out a phrase while the other is decidedly more abstract’: directly states the key difference between the two works in a single, focused sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — dates (1986 and 2013): emphasizes when the sculptures were made, not how they differ visually or conceptually. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘demonstrating the versatility of the popular material’: gestures at a difference but concludes with the artists’ shared use of neon, softening the contrast rather than emphasizing it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
